stimulus.content: 1765 — Stamp Act passed | 1770 — Boston Massacre | 1773 — Boston Tea Party | 1775 — Battles of Lexington and Concord | 1776 — Declaration of Independence signed | 1781 — Battle of Yorktown | 1783 — Treaty of Paris signed

stem: Based on the timeline, which event took place right before the Declaration of Independence?

choices.text: The Treaty of Paris was signed
The Battles of Lexington and Concord
The Battle of Yorktown was fought

answer_explanation: The timeline shows the Battles of Lexington and Concord in 1775 came right before the Declaration of Independence in 1776. The Boston Massacre was in 1770, and Yorktown and the Treaty of Paris came after.
